rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no monetary amounts present. Ink and lettering sharp and unambiguous.
Clear section heading directly under year. No amounts in the row.
Numbers align clearly with the three vertical ruling lines: 179 in pounds column, 4 in shillings, 10 pence. All values in valid ranges.

rows.description: Due under the old system by Bursar
rows.notes: Clear handwriting and alignment. Pounds = 50 sits in pounds column; shillings = 3, pence = 4 visible and unambiguous.

rows.description: Lovell 1/2 years Rent in lieu of repair
rows.notes: Text legible. Numeric group reads as 50 (pounds), 1 (shillings), 5 (pence). Minor ambiguity in the shape of the '1' (could be faint) but alignment with pounds column supports this reading.

rows.description: 10 per Cent on Rents of Estates
rows.notes: Three-part number clearly present and aligned to columns. The final digit (pence = 7) is slightly cramped but consistent with column alignment and other rows.

rows.description: 15 per Cent on Rent of Rooms
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings are clearly placed; pence digit is small but reads as '3' by alignment with the pence column. All values within valid ranges.
Small amount but digits (1, 4, 9) are clearly separated and sit under the respective pounds/shillings/pence columns. Good contrast and legibility.
Large pounds figure (185) is visible though the handwriting of the hundreds-digit is slightly ornate; shillings and pence read as 9 and 9. Chosen reading based on clear column alignment.
Digits 29 · 14 · 6 are well aligned under pounds/shillings/pence columns; handwriting crisp and unambiguous.

No pounds figure appears in the pounds column; the numeric pair '10 5' is aligned in the shillings and pence columns respectively (interpreted as 10s 5d). Slight uncertainty because the pounds column is blank but alignment is consistent.
Clear reading: 17 pounds, 11 shillings, 11 pence. All three digits line up properly with ruling lines; pence = 11 clearly formed.
Good legibility and alignment. Pounds 11, shillings 14, pence 4 are distinct and in valid ranges.
Numbers read as 2 (pounds), 2 (shillings), 4 (pence). The first '2' is small but sits in the pounds column; the shillings and pence are clear.

A single '3' is present to the right of the description. Its horizontal alignment is slightly ambiguous (could be in the pounds column or the shillings column). I have interpreted it as £3 0s 0d because it sits closer to the pounds column; however the small size and placement introduce moderate uncertainty.
Pounds '43' is clearly written and aligned in the pounds column. Shillings and pence are indicated as zeros (no figures present) and alignment supports this reading.

rows.description: Rectors moiety of Room Balance on
rows.notes: Clear triple number: 70 (pounds), 8 (shillings), 10 (pence). Characters are legible and fall under their respective columns.

rows.description: Allinett £6 towards repayment of Loan of £15
rows.notes: The entry explicitly names £6 and the numeric '6' sits in the pounds column. No shillings/pence shown. Text 'towards repayment of Loan of £15' is explanatory.

Line 'Loan of £15' appears as a descriptive line with no explicit numeric entry in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Possibly referenced by the previous line; absence of amounts is visible but the relationship is ambiguous.
Numeric '40' clearly placed in the pounds column; shillings and pence omitted (interpreted as zeros). Handwriting of the amount is crisp.
Final total boxed/ruled at page bottom (1659 pounds, 5 shillings, 9 pence). Alignment with vertical ruling lines is clear and digits are unambiguous.
